sections.paragraph:
ὕπανδρος, ον (ὑπό + ἀνήρ; Polyb. et al.; Cat. Cod. Astr. XIII 174, 9 al,; LXX; lit. ‘under the power of or subject to a man’) pert. to being legally bound to a man in marriage, married ἡ ὕπανδρος γυνή the married woman (Polyb. 10, 26, 3; Aelian, NA 3, 42; Artem. 1, 78 p. 74, 6; Athen. 9, 388c; Heliod. 10, 22; Num 5:20, 29; Pr 6:24, 29; Sir 9:9; 41:23; TestAbr B 12 p. 116, 14 [Stone p. 80]; Test12Patr) Ro 7:2.—M-M.

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

Aelian Aelian , II a.d.—List 5

Artem Artem , onir., II a.d.—List 5

Athen Athen , III a.d.—List 5

Heliod Heliod , III? a.d.—List 5

TestAbr TestAbr = Testament of Abraham, with some interpolations; I b.c./I a.d.—Lists 2, 5

Test12Patr Test12Patr = Testaments of the Twelve Patriarchs, with interpolations II b.c.–III a.d.—List 2

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1029
